email-subject: Payment Request Submitted for Powers Lab Online Research Study!
alert-message:
Hello! 

 

You are receiving this email because you consented to be contacted regarding your participation in an online research study by the Powers Lab. 

 

This email is to notify you that researchers have submitted a request for your $40 payment and is now being processed by the Yale Business office. 

 

Thank you so much for your participation :) 

 

You should receive your payment no later than 15 business days from now. The payment notification will be sent from gc-orders@amazon.com. Please check both your inbox and spam/junk folders to ensure the email is not filtered out. You will automatically receive another email asking you to confirm whether you have received your payment in 15 business days -- please to the survey attached in this email when you receive it :)

 

If at any time you have questions about your payment, then please reach out to maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu, CCing silmilly.toribio@yale.edu and albert.powers@yale.edu to inquire about the status of your payment!

 

If you opted for a digital US Bank prepaid card, then useful information is attached to this email. 

 

Thank you again, and have a fantastic day!

 

---------------------------------------------------------

Powers Lab | Yale University

Phone: ‪(203) 441-7213

Email: powers.lab@yale.edu

email-subject: Powers Lab at Yale Research Study: Inconsistent Answers
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you recently completed a Yale-approved online study conducted by the Powers lab and consented to be contacted regarding research.

 

As part of our commitment to conducting legitimate, high-quality science in order to develop treatments to help folks with mental illness, we carefully review all responses for consistency and accuracy. During our review, we noticed one or more major inconsistencies in your responses that will need to be resolved for us to use your data.

 

We realize mistakes happen and people sometimes select the wrong answer (especially in ours long, tedious questionnaire!), but we also have had a massive issue with folks taking the survey multiple times and giving very unreliable responses.

 

If you are confident that your responses were 100% honest, then please schedule Zoom call to clarify your responses at this Microsoft Bookings link. Please do not request a zoom call if you know the answers you provided were not honest and accurate. 

 

Thank you for your participation, and we apologize for any frustration!

---------------------------------------------------------

Powers Lab | Yale University

Phone: ‪(203) 441-7213

Email: powers.lab@yale.edu

email-subject: Powers Lab at Yale Research Study: Inconsistent Answers
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you 1) recently completed a Yale-approved online study conducted by the Powers lab, 2) consented to be contacted regarding research, and 3) gave inconsistent answers suspicious for inattentiveness/repeat survey taking. 

 

Because this is medical research, it is critical that we are collecting accurate, high-quality data (our data may impact patients!). 

 

Unfortunately, because numerous responses were inconsistent, we will have to assume that the data is not trustworthy and will not be able to use your data. As stated in our consent form, we will thus be unable to provide payment for your responses. 

 

Thank you for your participation, and we apologize for any frustration. 

---------------------------------------------------------

Powers Lab | Yale University

Phone: ‪(203) 441-7213

Email: powers.lab@yale.edu

email-subject: Powers Lab Online Study: Inconsistent Answers
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you 1) recently completed a Yale-approved online study conducted by the Powers lab, 2) consented to be contacted regarding research, and 3) gave inconsistent answers to some of our important questions that we will need to correct to be able to use your data.

 

In order to have your data used and receive your payment, please fill out this uber-brief (1-2 minutes) verification survey of basic demographic information

 

*******

[survey-link:clarification_survey:Answer Verification Link]

*******

 

This is not super uncommon, and usually it just means there was a mistake on a question or two! Transparently though, we have had a massive issue with fraud throughout the study, so if your answers are very suspicious (or if you fail to reply) we will have to assume that the data is not trustworthy and will need to discard your data and withhold payment :( 

 

We know mistakes happen, and we always always prefer to use folks' data if they are providing it in earnest and being honest, but we also owe it to everyone to conduct legitimate, rigorous science : )

 

If you would prefer to directly discuss the inconsistent answer(s) via a Zoom call, that is also an option! You can schedule a call with Max at the following link: 

https://outlook.office.com/book/YalePsychedelicStudy@yale.edu/s/ruvzBR9YwU6XkwVeneSjpw2?ismsaljsauthenabled 

 

Thank you very much for your participation and assistance with this!

 

---------------------------------------------------------

Powers Lab | Yale University

Phone: ‪(203) 441-7213

Email: powers.lab@yale.edu

Thank you again for your participation and patience! : )

email-subject: Powers Lab @ Yale Online Research Study: Ineligible
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you consented to be contacted regarding your research participation in an online study by the Powers Lab at Yale University.

 

Unfortunately, your responses in screening are highly suggestive of fraud, and so you will be unable to participate in this study.

 

If you are very confident that this email is being sent in error and still wish to participate, you may email maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu to double-check your responses. Unfortunately, due to high rates of fraud, we must stick to strict criteria to rule out any suspicious responses. Please understand we do not wish to comment on anyone's intention, but it is our responsibility to conduct honest, accurate scientific research.

  

Thank you for your interest, and we wish you the best!

email-subject: Powers Lab Online Research: Email Retraction
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you completed an online research study by the Powers Lab at Yale University and consented to be contacted regarding additional research opportunities.

 

It's come to our attention that while setting up automatic alerts for new participants, folks who already completed the study were mistakenly sent emails informing them that they would receive $40 for finishing the study within 15 business days. 

 

Our sincerest apologies -- your record was already processed back when you finished the study, and so you will not be receiving any bonus compensation :(  (even if we'd like to!) . 

 

Thank you again for your participation and patience! 

 

As always, please do not hesitate to reach out if you have questions or feedback!

 

P.S. If you would like to be removed from this email list, please just email maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu :)

We are committed to compensating you for your research in a timely fashion, and so we will be sending you digital VISA cards on Monday 7/7/2025 if we have not received an update from the Business office by the end of the day. 

 

We are so sorry! We earnestly try to get people paid as soon as possible for their participation and we are very surprised by this delay. We understand how anxiety-inducing it can be given that this study is entirely online! 

 

Thank you again so much for your participation, and please do not hesitate to reach out with questions or concerns!

 

Max

email-subject: Powers Lab Online Study: Reminder Confirmation and important participation information
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you consented to be contacted by the Powers Lab at Yale University regarding your participation in an online study, and you have indicated that you will be delaying your participation until [cb_continue_date] in order to participate in our baseline and/or longitudinal study.

 

Thank you so much! Your conscientious participation is extremely valuable to this research, and we welcome any suggestions to improve your research experience and/or better accommodate you.   

 

This email is to confirm receipt of your responses and that an email has been scheduled to remind you to participate anytime after [cb_continue_date]. If there is an earlier date that would be considerably more convenient please contact us and we will allow you to continue.

 

We also want to remind you of the key participation information: please refrain from cannabis use prior to days of participation. Specifically, we have determined cannabis will not interfere with our measures if you refrain for:

  • 3 days at a minimum
  • 1 week if using once per month
  • 2 weeks if using weekly or twice weekly
  • 4 weeks if using more than 3-4x weekly

 

Please let us know if you have questions or concerns! You may reach out to maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu at any time!

 

Thank you again!
